title: Cloud Security Posture Management Market Expected to Reach USD 15.62 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) market was valued at $5.96 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach $15.62 billion by 2035, growing at a 10.14% CAGR. The market expansion is driven by rapid cloud adoption outpacing manual security management capabilities, with cloud misconfiguration being the leading cause of breaches. North America leads the market with the U.S. representing $2.38 billion, while Europe is projected to reach $4.23 billion by 2035. Large enterprises and BFSI sectors dominate, though SMEs and healthcare are the fastest-growing segments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Recognized as one of the leading CSPM platform providers globally. The company's 2023 introduction of Prisma Cloud Darwin with AI-powered attack path analysis demonstrates continued innovation and competitive strength in the growing market.

title: Threat Intelligence Market: USD 18.85 Billion by 2031 with Strategic Intelligence Segment Accounting for 33.60% Revenue Share – Reports Mordor Intelligence
author: Mordor Intelligence
description: The global threat intelligence market is projected to grow from USD 10.38 billion in 2026 to USD 18.85 billion by 2031, registering a 12.7% CAGR. Growth is driven by rising cyberattacks, AI-powered threat detection adoption, and increasing demand for real-time cybersecurity monitoring across BFSI, healthcare, government, retail, and IT & telecom sectors.

title: Emerging AI-Driven Threats Prompt Renewed Focus on Enterprise Cybersecurity
author: Equity Insider
description: Following Anthropic's exposure of Claude Mythos, a frontier AI model that autonomously exploits zero-day vulnerabilities, the cybersecurity sector faces renewed pressure to modernize defenses. The incident triggered a 4.5% drop in the Global X Cybersecurity ETF. Major players including SentinelOne, Elastic, Rapid7, and Broadcom are advancing AI-powered security solutions, while quantum-safe encryption adoption remains critical as 91% of businesses lack formal migration roadmaps despite tightening compliance deadlines.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Expanded strategic partnership with Alphabet/Google Cloud across three regions; AI-native Singularity Platform positioned to replace legacy security tools; protects nearly one-fifth of Fortune 500 companies

Achieved FedRAMP High authorization unlocking access to sensitive government workloads; growing federal footprint with CISA collaboration and GSA discount agreement; supports cloud-native security operations

Acquired Kenzo Security to advance autonomous security investigations; customers report 94% reduction in investigation time and 100% alert coverage; enhances managed detection and response capabilities

Launched Symantec CBX unified detection and response platform combining Symantec and Carbon Black technologies; 85% of incident flags now carry AI-powered predictions; targets under-resourced security teams

title: Rapid7 Buys Kenzo Security To Launch Autonomous AI Security Operations
author: Akanksha Bakshi
description: Rapid7 acquired Kenzo Security to enhance its Command Platform with AI-driven cybersecurity operations. The acquisition enables automated alert investigation and correlation across multiple data sources, with customers reporting 94% reduction in investigation time. However, Rapid7 shares fell 2.28% in premarket trading Friday, trading 11% below its 20-day moving average and 34.5% below its 100-day moving average, reflecting an 80.15% decline over the past 12 months.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the Kenzo Security acquisition is strategically positive for long-term AI capabilities, the stock is experiencing significant downward pressure with a 2.28% premarket decline, 80.15% decline over 12 months, and trading well below key moving averages. The company carries a Hold rating with weak value metrics suggesting overvaluation concerns despite moderate growth prospects.

title: Rapid7 Acquires Kenzo Security to Accelerate Preemptive, AI-Powered Security Operations
author: Benzinga
description: Rapid7 has acquired Kenzo Security, an agentic AI platform designed to scale autonomous security investigations. The acquisition enhances Rapid7's Command Platform and MDR services, enabling organizations to investigate 100% of alerts at machine speed instead of the typical fraction. Kenzo customers reported a 94% reduction in investigation time and increased alert coverage from 12% to 100%. Rapid7 does not anticipate material impact to revenue, ARR, profitability, or free cash flows from this transaction.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The acquisition of Kenzo Security strengthens Rapid7's AI-powered security operations capabilities, enabling faster and more comprehensive alert investigations. The deal enhances their Command Platform and MDR services, positioning them as a leader in preemptive, AI-driven security operations. The company expects synergies and improved customer outcomes, though no material financial impact is anticipated in the near term.
